behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real recursive filesystem watching (OS event delivery + watcher configuration). A mocked watcher can miss bugs where subdirectory events are not emitted/propagated. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly targets real watch scope: "inside a subdirectory of the watched root".
behaviors.test_description: Run the real daemon against real temp input/output directories. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and assert one transformed output is produced without restart.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is module handoff/state propagation (event intake -> dedup window state -> enqueue/drop). No required real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected System because behavior text is path+time-window logic, not OS event semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake service, dedup module, queue, and a fake clock in-process. Submit two requests for identical path within window; assert first enqueued, second discarded, and window expiry allows later enqueue.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real OS event duplication. Mocking event streams can hide watcher/backend-specific duplication patterns. Rejected Integration due to explicit token: "operating system emits multiple filesystem events".
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon with real watcher on a temp directory. Perform one write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename or chunked append). Assert processing pipeline runs exactly once for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Requires real filesystem lifecycle events (delete/recreate) and watcher re-subscription behavior under OS semantics. In-process fakes can miss descriptor invalidation/rebind failures. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires runtime directory removal/recreation.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon continuously against a real watched path. Remove the directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file. Assert daemon detects and processes new file without restarting process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness depends on real filesystem timestamp behavior/resolution and concurrent arrival event ordering. Mocked timestamps/events can mask real ordering bugs. Rejected Integration because behavior text anchors ordering to "creation timestamps" under simultaneous arrivals.
behaviors.test_description: With real daemon and filesystem, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled creation times, then verify observed processing order matches timestamp order (or defined tie-breaker) in outputs/logs.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at real OS/filesystem write visibility and event timing boundary. Mocks usually cannot reproduce partial-write timing races faithfully. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly targets "still being written" timing against real arrival events.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on real watched directory. Open a file and write in chunks with delays while keeping writer active; trigger arrival event early. Assert daemon waits and processes only after final write/close, and output reflects complete file.
